Airframe Compatibility: Universal — would fit any; recommended only for budget builds (#4 DarwinFPV X9, #5 EMAX Hawk 7 if budget-constrained)
Notes: Budget GPS-only. Good for speed/distance OSD. No compass. 1Hz default needs Betaflight auto-config to 10Hz.

Airframe Compatibility: Universal — would fit any; but M10-class is strongly preferred over M8N for LR
Notes: Budget classic. Dual compass included but magnetometer largely useless on quads (EMI from power wires). Betaflight uses course-over-ground, not compass.

Airframe Compatibility: Best add-on GPS for any airframe that lacks it: #1 (MARK4 LR7), #2b (KOLAS7 PNP), #5 (EMAX Hawk 7), #7 (Crocodile75 V3), #8 (Chimera7 Pro V2), #9 (DarwinFPV 129 if needed), #4 (DarwinFPV X9)
Notes: Real u-blox M10. 4 constellations. Fast cold start. Supercap for hot start retention (vs battery on BN-880). Optimal balance price/performance.

Airframe Compatibility: Native iFlight: #3 (Chimera9 ECO — matches the +$39 GPS option), #8 (Chimera7 Pro V2 — officially listed as compatible)
Notes: iFlight official GPS. Mini version. Built-in farad cap for hot starts. Onboard LNA+RF filter+TCXO. DSC compass on I2C. Comes with TPU mount options.

Airframe Compatibility: Larger frames only: not required for any 7 candidate — probably overkill"
Notes: Standard size version. Larger patch antenna (25x25mm) for better signal. Same electronics as Mini.

Airframe Compatibility: Universal premium — overkill for this project vs G3 at $22-28. Only if existing equipment or deep preference for TBS ecosystem.
Notes: Premium option. Highest update rate (18Hz). No onboard compass (not needed for Betaflight). u.FL connector allows remote active antenna placement. Lightest.
